name: Limitless Pendant
short_description: A small clip-on wearable that continuously records and transcribes spoken conversations to power a personal AI with long-term memory.
long_description: Announced in April 2024 by Limitless (formerly Rewind AI), the Pendant is a lightweight magnetic-clasp device worn on the body that captures everyday speech, identifies who said what, and stores it as searchable, summarizable memory in the cloud. By moving capture off the desktop and onto the body, it lets a personalized AI assistant draw on what you have actually said and heard, generating meeting prep, automated notes, and recall on demand. It matters for physical AI in daily life as a near-formless, always-on audio agent that turns ambient conversation into a queryable autobiographical record, while raising clear questions about consent and continuous recording.

images.caption: The Limitless Pendant, a clip-on AI wearable, shown beside the app's automated meeting notes.
images.alt_text: A small round black wearable device with a magnetic clasp, next to a screenshot of an app showing meeting prep notes and a transcript.

sources.title: Limitless
sources.access_notes: Official product page for the Limitless Pendant.

sources.title: a16z-backed Rewind pivots to build AI-powered pendant to record your conversations
sources.access_notes: Covers the April 2024 rebrand from Rewind to Limitless and the Pendant announcement.

interactions.title: Ask what was actually said
interactions.description: After a meeting, open the app and type "what price did Jennifer quote for the GPU order?" and the assistant pulls the exact line out of the day's recordings instead of you scrolling a transcript.
interactions.source_label: Limitless Help Center — Search, Ask AI, Summaries

interactions.title: Learns who's who in 20 seconds
interactions.description: Label a person's voice once with about twenty seconds of audio, and the Pendant automatically attributes their lines in every later conversation, even when people switch between languages.

interactions.title: A white light says it's listening
interactions.description: When the Pendant is recording, a white LED stays lit so the person across from you can see it, and they can ask the wearer to switch it off — the device's built-in answer to recording people who didn't sign up for it.
interactions.source_label: Limitless Help Center — talking to a Pendant wearer

interactions.title: Think out loud on a walk
interactions.description: Reviewers stopped using it only for meetings and started narrating half-formed ideas while walking or driving, treating the Pendant as a capture device for the business idea you had three weeks ago and forgot.

interactions.title: Wired into a journaling pipeline
interactions.description: One user automated the lifelog so each evening it generated a journal entry in Google Docs with the day's key moments and decisions, and routed open questions to Perplexity for research, emailed back the next morning.

interactions.title: Catching a kid's first 'I love you'
interactions.description: Because it records all day, owners describe the Pendant accidentally preserving ordinary domestic moments — a child's first "I love you" — or scrubbing timestamps to figure out exactly when last night's power outage hit.

interactions.title: Action items land in your task app
interactions.description: Ask the assistant for the commitments from a call and it returns a clean, importable list of who-owes-what that drops straight into a to-do app or calendar with no reformatting.

interactions.title: Settle the 'you never told me' argument
interactions.description: Since it timestamps and attributes who said what, a wearer could quietly resolve a domestic disagreement by recalling whether a chore or plan was actually agreed to — grounded in the device's all-day capture and speaker diarization, though the consent rules cut both ways at home.
